questions.context: In the diagram, AB is parallel to CD. T is a point between the lines such that AT and CT are line segments. Angle BAT = 55° and angle DCT = 40°.

parts.label: a
parts.questionText:
Work out angle ATC.
Give full reasons for each step.

markScheme.desc: Draw line XY through T parallel to AB and CD

workingSteps: Draw line XY through T parallel to AB and CD

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Work out angle TAB, given that angle TAC = 30°.

markScheme.desc: In triangle ATC: angles sum to 180°; ATC = 95°, TAC = 30°, so TCA = 55°
Angle TAB = angle BAT − angle TAC... use angle BAT = 55°; so TAB is part of BAT

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Prove that the angle sum of any triangle is 180°, using the property of parallel lines.
parts.correctAnswer: Draw line through vertex parallel to opposite side; use alternate angles.

markScheme.desc: Draw line through one vertex parallel to the opposite side
Use alternate angles to show the three angles form a straight line (180°)

workingSteps: In triangle ABC, draw line XY through A parallel to BC.
Angle XAB = angle ABC (alternate angles, XY ∥ BC)
Angle YAC = angle ACB (alternate angles, XY ∥ BC)
XAB + BAC + YAC = 180° (angles on straight line XY)
Therefore angle ABC + angle BAC + angle ACB = 180°

questions.context: PQRS is a quadrilateral where PQ = QR, angle QPR = angle QRP and PS ∥ QR.

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Prove that triangle PQR is isosceles.
parts.correctAnswer: PQ = QR given, OR angle QPR = angle QRP so PQ = QR by converse isosceles theorem.

markScheme.desc: A triangle with two equal sides is isosceles oe

workingSteps: A triangle with two equal sides (PQ = QR) is isosceles by definition.

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Given PS ∥ QR, show that PQRS is a trapezium.
parts.correctAnswer: PS ∥ QR by given; one pair of parallel sides defines a trapezium.

markScheme.desc: A quadrilateral with exactly one pair of parallel sides is a trapezium

workingSteps: A quadrilateral with one pair of parallel sides is a trapezium.

parts.label: c
parts.questionText:
Given additionally that angle PQR = 70°, work out angle QPS.
Give a reason.
workingSteps: PS ∥ QR, so QPS and PQR are co-interior angles
